Selected Work

Behind the Vision

I work with luxury and premium brands to shape how they're perceived long before they're seen.

From strategy to execution, every decision is designed to create clarity, consistency, and lasting value.

i

One thing I've come to believe.

Every meaningful project begins long before execution.

ii

A thought I keep coming back to.

Before strategy comes observation.

iii

Something I've noticed.

The strongest brands don't try to be remembered. They build something worth remembering.

iv

Something worth protecting.

Every decision is an opportunity to protect what makes a brand unmistakably itself.

v

Something I won't compromise.

Values before visibility. Always.

Brand Strategy

Defining what should remain before deciding what should change.

Market Intelligence

Understanding people before positioning brands.

Production Direction

Protecting quality from concept to execution.

Experience Systems

Designing consistency across every interaction.

Case Study 001

Cho(co)islate
CASE 01 Luxury Lingerie · Brand Repositioning · Premium Market

Chocolate
Lingerie

Three years of silence.
One strategic reset.

A luxury lingerie brand with a product worthy of the premium market—but the wrong language, the wrong audience, and three years of silence.

The challenge wasn't creating a better brand.
It was revealing the one that was already there.

Market Intelligence Client Profiling Brand Repositioning Campaign Direction Content Strategy

Luxury wasn't missing.
Direction was.

01 — Diagnosis

The brand had the product.
It just hadn't learned to speak yet.

Consistent brand presence.

02 — The Field

Six brands compete for visibility. Only one could own quiet desire.

Eres

"The most powerful thingsare never fully revealed."

Every strategy begins with a question.

Beyond
the Product

A Perspective on Fashion Marketing

What allows a luxury Maison to remain desirable beyond the product itself?

What I Observed

Fashion Marketing doesn't simply communicate
a Maison's identity.
It shapes how that identity
is experienced.

Individual experiences may create desire.
Connected experiences allow it to endure.

Creating continuity in moments.

Because the strongest luxury brands don't simply create desire.
They design the conditions for it to endure.

Maison Identity

Intent

Experience

Memory

Relationship

Enduring Desirability

If desirability is built through connected experiences rather than isolated moments, then every stage of the client journey becomes an opportunity to strengthen the relationship.

Before

During

After

Proposal I

Narrative Brief

Aligning intention before execution.

Every activation could begin with a Narrative Brief, aligning every team around the same emotional intention before execution begins.

Strategic Rationale

When every team begins with the same intention, every client touchpoint has the opportunity to feel like part of the same Maison story.

intention before execution

the same Maison story

Proposal II

Memory Mapping

Client journeys could be designed to extend conversations rather than conclude them.

Traditional event KPIs could be complemented with qualitative insights into the moments clients remember, revisit and share.

Strategic Rationale

Operational metrics reveal what happened. Memories reveal what truly remained. Understanding both creates a more complete picture of the client experience.

Operational metrics reveal what happened. Memories reveal what truly remained.

Guests stayed longer in the personalization room.

Conversations centered around craftsmanship and details.

Most photographed moment.

Strong emotional response to the story behind the collection.

“The atmosphere felt intimate, not like a traditional show.”

“The conversation with the team made it personal.”

“The details — the music, the pacing, the setting.”

Traditional KPIs explain performance. Memory explains meaning.

Proposal III

Relationship Continuity

Designing every interaction as the next chapter of the same story.

Strategic Proposal

Post-event client journeys could intentionally build on previous interactions, allowing every touchpoint to feel like the next chapter of the same relationship.

Strategic Rationale

Luxury relationships are rarely defined by a single moment. They are built through continuity, where each experience naturally extends the one before it.

The most meaningful relationships aren’t created in a single moment. They’re strengthened every time the story continues.

Client Journey Blueprint

01 · Invitation

02 · Boutique Visit

03 · Private Event

04 · Follow-Up

05 · Private Preview

06 · Appointment

07 · Next Collection

The story begins with an invitation.

A personalized welcome into the world of CHANEL.

Shared moments that create connection and belonging.

Thoughtful follow-up that makes the client feel known.

Exclusive previews that bring them closer to what's next.

Personal guidance that turns preference into confidence.

The relationship evolves, and the story continues.

Proposal

Client journeys could be designed to continue conversations rather than conclude them.

Internal Concept

Every touchpoint adds a layer.

Every layer strengthens the story.

This is the perspective I would bring to Fashion Marketing.

Creating continuity in moments.

Because the strongest luxury brands don't simply create desire.

They design the conditions for it to endure.
